zh.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede是Jan I van der Lede和Jutta的女兒，於1235年出生在荷蘭武特雷特。她和Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV至少有3個兒子和1個女兒。她去世於1291年12月30日在荷蘭武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede是Jan I van der Lede和Jutta的女儿，于1235年出生在荷兰武特雷特。她和Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV至少有3个儿子和1个女儿。她去世于1291年12月30日在荷兰武特雷特易瑟爾斯坦享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: der
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Hungarian Zoltan, Istvan, Janos, Lajos.
Hungarian (Dér): from the old personal name Dér of Turkic origin, or a nickname for a gray-haired man, from the vocabulary word dér ‘hoarfrost’.
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 謝, see   Xie   1.

variants.name: Xie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Hong, Li, Ming, Ying, Min, Qing, Yuan, Bin, Hui, Ping, Wei, Xiaoli, Ding, Chang, Lan, Quan, Cong, Dang, Hai, Tian, Yiping.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 謝, meaning ‘thank’ in Chinese: (i) from Xie (謝), the name of an ancient state (possibly located in Nanyang in Henan province), annexed around the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ).Later this place was probably granted to Shen Bo (the Earl of Shen), uncle of King Xuan of Zhou (reigned 827–782  BC ). After it was annexed during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ), the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Zhi-Le (直勒) by Xie Xiaozheng (謝孝政), an official during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Lede
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Bartolo, Croce, Dante, Domenico, Giorgio, Luigi, Pietro, Salvatore, Vincenzo, Vito.  French/Haitian Guerline, Jean Marie.
Italian: from the female personal name Fede, from Latin fides ‘faith’.
Italian: from a short form of the personal name   Federico  .

variants.name: Federico
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Antonio, Guerino, Armando, Salvatore, Domenico, Gino, Mario, Nino, Sal, Carmine, Guido, Alfredo, Angelo, Benito, Carmela.  Spanish Jose, Ruben, Carlos, Jesus, Rosario, Enrique, Francisco, Juan.
Italian: from the personal name Federico, of ancient Germanic origin (see Friedrich ). Compare Federick and Frederico .

variants.name: Di Fede
meanings: Italian (Sicily): patronymic or metronymic from the male or female personal name   Fede  .

nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Johannes ( see John ).

variants.name: Johannah
meanings: feminine form of Johannes ( see John ).

variants.name: Jonna
meanings: contracted form of   Johanna .

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

en.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede was born in 1235, in Utrecht, Netherlands as the daughter of Jan I van der Lede and Jutta. She had at least 3 sons and 1 daughter with Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV. She died on 30 December 1291, in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Netherlands, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede est née en 1235, à Utrecht, Pays-Bas, fille de Jan I van der Lede et Jutta. Elle a eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille avec Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV. Elle est décédée le 30 décembre 1291, à IJsselstein, Utrecht, Pays-Bas, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede wurde 1235 in Utrecht, Niederlande, als Tochter von Jan I van der Lede und Jutta geboren. Sie hatte mit Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 30. Dezember 1291 im Alter von 56 Jahren in IJsselstein, Utrecht, Niederlande.

it.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede è nata il nel 1235 nel Utrecht, Paesi Bassi come figlia di Jan I van der Lede e Jutta. Lei avuto almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia con Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV. Morì il 30 dicembre 1291, nel IJsselstein, Utrecht, Paesi Bassi, all'età di 56 anni.

ja.IJsselstein, Utrecht, Netherlands: オランダウトレチトイジセルステイン
ja.IJsselstein, Utrecht, Nederland: オランダウトレチトイジセルステイン
ja.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Ledeは1235年にオランダウトレチトでJan I van der LedeとJuttaの娘として生まれた。彼女はGijsbrecht Van Amstel IVとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1291年12月30日にオランダウトレチトイジセルステインで56歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede(은)는 1235년 네덜란드 우트레ch트에서 Jan I van der Lede(와)과 Jutta의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1291년 12월 30일 네덜란드 우트레ch트 이j셀스타인에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede nasceu em 1235, em Utrecht, Países Baixos filha de Jan I van der Lede e Jutta. Ela teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha com Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV. Ela faleceu em 30 de dezembro de 1291, em IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Baixos, com 56 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede родилась в 1235 г. в местности утречт, Нидерланды. Её родителей звали Jan I van der Lede и Jutta. У неё было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь от Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV. Она умерла 30 декабря 1291 г. в местности иджсселстин, утречт, Нидерланды в возрасте 56 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Johanna van der Lede nació en 1235, en Utrecht, Países Bajos como hija de Jan I van der Lede y Jutta. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija con Gijsbrecht Van Amstel IV. Murió el 30 de diciembre de 1291, en IJsselstein, Utrecht, Países Bajos, a la edad de 56 años.
